body.title: IGOR_SINGLE_TICKET env var — restrict pe_chain to one named ticket during cert
body.result: _enforce_single_ticket_mode in pe_entry_init reads IGOR_SINGLE_TICKET env var. Set → only that ticket passes ENTRY; unset/empty → normal autonomous pickup. 10 tests, 4203 regression pass. TODO comment marks for removal after cc-walk-10 cert complete.
body.description:
Add IGOR_SINGLE_TICKET env var checked in pe_chain. When set to a ticket id, pe_chain only claims that ticket; all others are skipped with a log line ('[pe_chain] single-ticket mode: only T-x may be claimed; skipping T-y'). Unset/empty → normal autonomous pickup (current behavior, zero overhead).

**Context:** the cert protocol for T-cc-walk-01..10 requires CC to single-step Igor through one ticket at a time, validate the end product, and mark complete only when Igor produces real work autonomously. Without this switch, Igor's pe_chain greedily picks any worker=igor ticket from the queue — which has been undermining the protocol for 5 days.

**HIGH-inertia note:** pe_chain.py is load-bearing. Akien pre-approved this design inline in conversation (2026-04-29). Switch is intentionally temporary.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (check in pe_entry_init or pe_claim) + tests/test_pe_chain_single_ticket_mode.py.
**Design rules:** approach-frame (positive: 'pe_chain only claims the named ticket'). No-sqlite. Test-plan present.
**Scope boundary:** in scope: env var read + filter logic + log line + tests. Out of scope: a sentinel-file alternative, multi-ticket allowlist, queue.json mutation.
**Test plan:** unset → ticket selection unchanged (regression); set to claimable id → only that one claims, others skipped + logged; set to nonexistent id → all skipped (no claim possible); set + ticket appears later → claimable when it does. Add # TODO: remove after cc-walk-10 cert complete comment in source.
**Pre-approval (HIGH-inertia):** Akien stamped 2026-04-29 — pe_chain change for cert protocol scaffolding.
